name: Ryōichi Hattori
description: {{nihongo|'''Ryōichi Hattori'''|服部 良一|Hattori Ryōichi|October 1, 1907 – January 30, 1993}} was a Japanese pop and jazz composer. [[Katsuhisa Hattori]] is his son. He had a great influence on Japanese pop and was awarded the [[People's Honor Award]]. [[Japanese jazz]] was downtrodden during [[World War II]], but he created a jazz boom after the war. He composed many songs for various artists such as [[Noriko Awaya]], [[Shizuko Kasagi]], [[Ichimaru]] and [[Ichirō Fujiyama]]. He also composed [[Li Xianglan]]'s song {{nihongo|"Suzhou Nocturne" |蘇州夜曲|Soshū yakyoku}}, which has remained controversial in China despite not being a militaristic song.
